title: Jade Buchanan
content:
People.ContactEmail jbuchanan@mccarthy.ca
- British Columbia, 2017
- Dalhousie University
Focused on privacy and technology law but even more focused on solutions and efficiency, clients describe Jade as “results-oriented and very responsive” and “down to earth”.
With rankings from Chambers and Lexpert, and recognition as a 2024 Lexpert Rising Star and a 2021 Global Data Review 40 Under 40, Jade is one of British Columbia’s most recognized privacy and technology lawyers.

#### Success in Technology Projects
Jade’s goal is to help our clients develop, acquire and deploy cutting-edge technology. He brings together all aspects of his practice to identify and mitigate key risks particularly cybersecurity and privacy—and successfully negotiate commercial contracts. He has led the negotiation of dozens of major information technology project deals and has particular experience in the acquisition of cybersecurity products and SaaS solutions. As counsel to Canada’s Digital Technology Supercluster, Jade has helped launch several multi-participant technology development projects that bring together the private and public sector.

#### A Leader in Cybersecurity and Privacy
Jade’s cybersecurity practice begins before the incident, helping organizations with incident preparedness, risk management and the acquisition of cybersecurity software and services. He has worked on some of the largest cybersecurity incidents in Canadian history and dozens of other incidents, including data breaches and ransomware attacks.
On privacy compliance, Jade has particular experience in the retail, financial and technology sectors, helping clients balance compliance, risk, innovation and competitiveness. Jade has a strong interest in leading the rapid development of privacy and cybersecurity law. He recently appeared before the B.C.’s Legislative Assembly’s Special Committee to review the Personal Information Protection Act . Jade has spoken on technology, privacy and cybersecurity at dozens of events and guest lectured at the University of Victoria and for Osgoode Hall Law School’s Certificates in Privacy and Cybersecurity Law and E-Discovery, Information Governance and Privacy.

#### Unique Public Sector Experience
As part of his unique public sector focus, Jade has extensive experience advising on British Columbia’s public sector privacy and access legislation (the Freedom of Information and Protection of Privacy Act ) for public bodies and their service providers. He has led the negotiation of hundreds of millions of dollars worth of public sector technology contracts and outsourcing and understands the unique privacy, cybersecurity and commercial needs of the public sector. From advising on records management to managing complex FOI requests to supporting judicial review of the Information and Privacy Commissioner, Jade provides comprehensive advice on the FOI process.

#### Advancing Legal Technology
Supporting technology adoption in the practice of law, Jade helped found MT>Forge and works closely with MT>3 on using technology, people and processes to provide efficient solutions to legal problems. Jade regularly provides on-going education within the firm, to clients and to the broader legal community on how to improve the practice of law using technology.
Jade in a member of the Law Society of British Columbia and Law Society of Saskatchewan (inactive). Jade clerked for the Saskatchewan Court of Appeal under the Chief Justice.
